diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient initially presented with characteristics of NS at birth, including facial dysmorphisms and pulmonary valve stenosis. Over time, the development of multiple lentigines, sensorineural deafness, and hypertrophic cardiomyopathy suggested LS. Later findings of generalized hypomineralization, cyst-like lesions in the mandible, and low osseous maturation led to the diagnosis of NLS. Molecular analysis identified a novel PTPN11 gene mutation (Ala461Thr), linking these phenotypes to a unique genetic defect.
